title: Woodsia × gracilis
intro: Woodsia × gracilis, also known as hybrid woodsia fern, is a rare and unique plant species that belongs to the family Woodsiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation and respiratory issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Woodsia × gracilis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and coumarins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal preparations, Woodsia × gracilis is often combined with other plants to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that are used to treat respiratory problems and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
taxonomy: Woodsia × gracilis is a hybrid of two species of ferns, classified under the genus Woodsia. The genus Woodsia belongs to the family Woodsiaceae, which is a part of the order Polypodiales. Woodsia × gracilis is further classified as a member of the family Polypodiopsida, a division of vascular plants. This classification is based on its characteristics, such as its leaf morphology and reproductive structures. As a hybrid species, Woodsia × gracilis is not considered to be a distinct species, but rather a unique combination of its parent species.
